# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python project. Install dependencies using pip install -r requirements.txt without hash verification. We want to stay current with the latest package versions. The workflow should run tests and deploy to our servers.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD GitHub Actions Workflow for Python project (Install dependencies, Run Tests & Deploy) 
on: [push] # set this to 'pull_request' if you want it run when code is pushed into a branch. Make sure the workflow file has access right on your repository and permissions are granted appropriately in GitHub actions settings. For production deployment remove "or" clause before pushing changes (in case of any sensitive data)
jobs:  # replace 'matrix jobs' with what you like to call it, ie how often does this job run? can be once or every xxx schedule... etc   eg matrix(Python version), python3.8,...,python versions and pull requests/checks if they exist for each trigger in the workflow file
  build: # replace 'Build' with whatever you want to call it, egs test-build; make sure job name is unique per repository or organization (the repo must have CI jobs enabled)   eg. If your org has many repos then specify a more specific one which also includes that in the same comment
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest # replace this with what you like and why, egs based on cloud provider if needed... etc; make sure it matches up to where workflows are run from   eg Linux/Mac or Windows 10 Docker Container (the os specified)     If we use a different OS for testing than the main one then remove "or" clause
    steps: # replace 'stages' with what you like, egs preparing; compiling... etc. Add as many stages per job comment if there are multiple or just move them over to separate comments   eg Step 1 - Install python3 and pip (to install packages), step2- ...etc..
    - name: Setup Python env # replace 'Python' with whatever you like, egs setup_python; use specific version when needed... etc. Add as many steps per job comment if there are multiple or just move them over to separate comments   eg Step 1 (setup python), step2,...etc
    - uses: actions/checkout@v2 # replace 'CheckOut' with whatever you like, egs Checking out code source... etc. Add as many steps per job comment if there are multiple or just move them over to separate comments   eg Step 1 (checking Out Code), step2,...etc
    - name: Install dependencies using pip install-r requirements # replace 'pip' with whatever you like, egs Python packages installation...... etc. Add as many steps per job comment if there are multiple or just move them over to separate comments   eg Step 1 (install_dependencies), step2,...etc
